summary: Frontend re-verification of the 5 fixes from iteration_18. Sc1d (Restoration Eligibility auto-save) and Sc2 (+Invoice modal opens in-place / backdrop guard / X-close / Save persistence) now FULLY PASS. Sc-Digest dry-run API PASSES with correct response shape. The Send-Digest UI button could not be exercised on the dashboard because the 7/14/30d thresholds currently surface 0 stale deals (data has aged out — confirmed via API for all 3 thresholds). Sc3 (Record Payment) is PARTIALLY working: modal reopens prepopulated with the oldest unpaid invoice (invoice_number visible in header), amount_paid persists to backend with balance_due=0, BUT the invoice status remains 'Draft' instead of auto-flipping to 'Paid'. This is a new backend regression that was masked while the modal-open bug existed.
tests_executed: Sc2 PASS: Click [data-testid=quick-new-invoice] on deal 1fab3a5c (Acme Office, $62,000) — [data-testid=invoice-editor] opens, URL unchanged at /projects/1fab3a5c. Line item textarea value = 'Acme Office — Re-roof — Contract'. Total = $62,000.
Sc2 PASS backdrop: Click on the dark backdrop area (outside white card) closes the modal — the e.target===e.currentTarget guard is working.
Sc2 PASS X-close: Click the × button in the modal header closes it.
Sc2 PASS Save: Click [data-testid=save-invoice] persists invoice. GET /api/invoices?deal_id=... returns count=1 (INV-2026-1218, status=Draft, total=62000.0). Cleanup via DELETE succeeded.
Sc2 MINOR: Success toast wording is 'Created INV-2026-1218' (line 553 in Invoices.jsx), NOT 'Invoice saved' as the task spec wrote. Functionality is correct; only wording differs.
Sc3 PASS (modal): Click [data-testid=quick-record-payment] — invoice-editor reopens and the just-created invoice_number 'INV-2026-1218' is visible in the modal header (prepopulation works).
Sc3 PASS (amount_paid persistence): Fill [data-testid=amount-paid]=62000 + Save -> GET /api/invoices/<id> returns amount_paid=62000.0, balance_due=0.0.

Sc3 FAIL (status auto-Paid): After amount_paid=total and Save, status remains 'Draft' instead of flipping to 'Paid'. Backend has the auto-status logic at server.py:2140 (`if inv['balance_due'] <= 0.01: status = 'Paid'`) but it does not appear to fire on the editor's PUT /api/invoices/{id} path — likely the editor is sending the explicit 'Draft' status field which the PUT handler trusts verbatim instead of recomputing.

Sc-Digest API dry-run PASS: POST /api/dashboard/stale-deals/digest?days=1&dry_run=true -> {owners_eligible:1, sent:0, digests:[{owner_email:'admin@roofingcrm.com', stuck_count:2, no_deposit_count:0, subject:'Stale Deals Digest — 2 need your attention'}]}. All required keys present.

Sc-Digest UI button: At default threshold 7d the dashboard shows [data-testid=stale-deals-empty] banner (no stale deals) and no [data-testid=send-stale-digest] button. Same at 14d and 30d (API confirms deals=0, counts={stuck:0,no_deposit:0} for all 3 thresholds). The UI does not expose a 1d option, so the button cannot be exercised in this environment until data ages further or a sub-7d threshold is added. Per the task's other_misc_info, API dry-run was used as the verification path and PASSED.

Sc1d PASS: POST /api/assessments {} -> id N. Visit /assessments/N, click [data-testid=insulation-saturated] and [data-testid=structural-deck-damaged]. Wait 1.5s. GET /api/assessments/N returns insulation_saturated=true, structural_deck_damaged=true. Reload page; both checkboxes still checked on server and in UI. Cleanup via DELETE succeeded. updateAndSave() in AssessmentEditor.jsx:191 is correctly wired and persisting on toggle.

critical.issue: When the InvoiceEditor sends a payload with amount_paid >= total (balance_due=0), the saved status is whatever the editor passed (Draft for newly-created invoices) — the server does NOT auto-flip to 'Paid'. server.py:2140 has the right rule (`if inv['balance_due'] <= 0.01: status = 'Paid'`) but it only runs on specific code paths (probably the dedicated payment-recording endpoint), not on the generic invoice PUT used by the modal Save button. Result: Sc3 leaves a 'paid in full' invoice stuck at Draft.

integration_issues.issue: amount_paid is saved correctly and balance_due reaches 0, but the invoice never transitions to status='Paid' after the user records full payment via the modal. Suggest either: (a) server-side: in the PUT handler, recompute status from amount_paid/total before saving; OR (b) client-side: in InvoiceEditor.save() when amount_paid >= total, set form.status='Paid' before submitting (matches the dropdown logic likely used elsewhere).

Dashboard threshold options are fixed at 7/14/30 days. In the current preview environment there are 0 stale deals at all three thresholds, so [data-testid=send-stale-digest] is never rendered. The send-digest UI flow (window.confirm preview -> POST cc_admin=true -> success toast) therefore cannot be exercised end-to-end here. Consider adding a smaller threshold option (e.g., 1d/3d) or a debug 'force show' toggle so admins can verify the digest button in test environments where data has aged out.

action_items: HIGH: Fix the 'status remains Draft after full payment' regression on PUT /api/invoices/{id}. Either recompute status server-side from amount_paid/total, or set form.status='Paid' inside InvoiceEditor.save() in Invoices.jsx when amount_paid>=total. Otherwise the Record-Payment quick action does not actually mark the invoice paid even though it stores the cash.

MINOR: Either (a) update task spec wording to say 'Created <invoice_number>' / 'Invoice updated', or (b) change Invoices.jsx:553/557 toast to 'Invoice saved' for a uniform message — the task spec expected 'Invoice saved'.
OPTIONAL: Expose a 1d or 3d threshold option in the Dashboard StaleDeals widget (or a debug 'show even if empty' toggle) so the send-digest UI button can be tested when production data ages out.
critical_code_review_comments: Invoices.jsx:569-574 backdrop guard `if (e.target === e.currentTarget) onClose()` is correctly implemented — Sc2 modal-open intermittence is resolved.

AssessmentEditor.jsx:191-199 updateAndSave correctly performs an immediate PUT on every checkbox toggle. Note: it sends the *entire* doc on every keystroke/toggle, which is fine for two boolean checkboxes but if extended to more fields would benefit from debouncing (e.g., 500ms) to avoid request storms.

server.py:2140 has the right `if balance_due<=0.01 then status='Paid'` rule, but it is NOT applied uniformly on PUT /api/invoices/{id}. The PUT handler should recompute status from the freshly-computed balance_due before persisting, otherwise the editor must remember to set status='Paid' manually — which it currently does not.

Dashboard.jsx:611-649 sendDigest() correctly does the dry-run preview -> window.confirm -> live POST(cc_admin=true) -> toast sequence. The button visibility is conditional on `total > 0`, which is correct.

success_rate.backend: Sc-Digest API: PASS; Invoice PUT auto-status: FAIL
success_rate.frontend: Sc2 PASS, Sc3 modal-open PASS (status flip FAIL), Sc1d PASS, Send-Digest UI: not exercisable (data state)

seed_data_creation: Temporarily created and then deleted: 1 invoice on deal 1fab3a5c (Acme Office), 1 empty assessment. Deal 1fab3a5c is back to 0 invoices after run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All three iteration-18 fixes (Invoices.jsx backdrop guard, DealDetail inline InvoiceEditor wiring, AssessmentEditor updateAndSave auto-save) are verified working. The remaining failure is a backend regression: PUT /api/invoices/{id} does not flip status to Paid when amount_paid>=total. Reproduce by creating any invoice via the +Invoice modal (Draft), then opening the same invoice via Record Payment, setting amount-paid=total, and saving. GET /api/invoices/{id} will show balance_due=0 but status='Draft'. server.py:2140 has the desired rule — it just isn't reached on this PUT path. Note: PostHog & emergent-main script tags break direct page.evaluate(fetch(...)); use page.context.request (NOT awaited) for API calls from playwright. Send-Digest UI button cannot be exercised until data ages further or a sub-7d threshold is added — verify via API days=1 dry-run instead.
rca of the issue: Sc3 status-flip bug RCA: The InvoiceEditor save() function (Invoices.jsx ~545-555) POSTs/PUTs the `form` object verbatim, including its current `status` field. For a brand-new invoice the form starts with status='Draft' and the user has no reason to change it when they just want to record a payment — they only adjust amount_paid. The backend PUT handler then stores status='Draft' verbatim. The auto-status rule at server.py:2140 (`if balance_due<=0.01: status='Paid'`) exists but lives in a different code path (likely the explicit payment_milestone or transition handler), so it doesn't fire for plain PUTs. Fix options: (1) server-side — recompute status from amount_paid/total inside the PUT handler before persisting; OR (2) client-side — in Invoices.jsx save(), set form.status='Paid' when Number(form.amount_paid)>=Number(form.total) - 0.01 before sending. Option (1) is more robust because it also handles direct API consumers.
